XOnlineMatchSearchGetResults

Retrieves the results from a previous call to XOnlineMatchSearch or XOnlineMatchSessionFindFromID.

HRESULT XOnlineMatchSearchGetResults(
    XONLINETASK_HANDLE hTask,
    PXONLINE_MATCH_SEARCHRESULT **prgpSearchResults,
    DWORD *pdwReturnedResults
);

Parameters

hTask
[in] Handle to the online task that was returned by XOnlineMatchSearch or XOnlineMatchSessionFindFromID.
prgpSearchResults

[out] Pointer to a pointer that receives the address of an array of pointers to search results. For example, the expression prgpSearchResults[0] is a pointer to an XONLINE_MATCH_SEARCHRESULT structure that represents the first search result. Following each XONLINE_MATCH_SEARCHRESULT structure is the variable-length attribute data for the session.

For each search result, call the XOnlineMatchSearchParse function to parse the attributes for that search result.

pdwReturnedResults

[out] Address of a DWORD variable that receives the number of search results—that is, the number of pointers in the array pointed to by the prgpSearchResults parameter.

Return Values

If XOnlineMatchSearchGetResults succeeds, the return value is S_OK.

If XOnlineMatchSearchGetResults fails, the return value is one of the following error codes.

Value Description
XONLINE_E_MATCH_SERVER_ERROR A server error occurred that is not correctable from the client. More detailed error information is returned by XOnlineTaskContinue when the search completes. If this error occurs during development, inform Microsoft of the error. If this error occurs during a game, ask the player to try again later.
Other See Online Errors for information about other possible errors.

Remarks

The XOnlineMatchSearchGetResults function is called from an Xbox that wishes to join an online-game session that is hosted by a different Xbox. For information on how to connect to a particular online-game session, see the Xbox Secure Network Library white paper.

The attributes returned in a search result are not necessarily the same as the attributes that were advertised by the host Xbox in the calls to XOnlineMatchSessionCreate and XOnlineMatchSessionUpdate.

No session attributes are available if the hTask parameter specifies a task that was returned from the XOnlineMatchSessionFindFromID function. To obtain attributes from a specific session, you must specify a task that was returned from the XOnlineMatchSearch function.

To obtain Quality of Service (QoS) information for matchmaking results, use the following functions:

  • XNetQosListen
  • XNetQosLookup
  • XNetQosRelease

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.

See Also

Playing Games with Matchmaking | Xbox Live Functions | XOnlineMatchSessionFindFromID, XOnlineMatchSearch, XOnlineMatchSearchParse
